title: Electrical Basics Explorer
summary: Learners explore charge movement, complete circuits, and the difference between electron flow and conventional current inside a simple bench-top 3D training space.
learningObjectives: Identify the source, conductor, load, and return path in a complete circuit.
Compare electron flow and conventional current without changing circuit behavior.
Recognize how open and closed paths affect current movement.

environment.setting: Foundations training lab with battery bench, lamp circuit, and material sample wall
environment.focus: Core electrical concepts before calculations or field installation

interactables.label: Battery and switch
interactables.teaches: Shows how voltage creates a potential difference that drives charge through a closed path.

interactables.label: Flow direction toggle
interactables.teaches: Switches between electron flow and conventional current arrows using the same circuit.

interactables.label: Conductor and insulator samples
interactables.teaches: Highlights why copper carries current while plastic insulation does not.

interactables.label: Open circuit break point
interactables.teaches: Demonstrates why the lamp turns off when the circuit path is interrupted.

tasks: Close the switch and confirm the lamp energizes.
Toggle the flow overlay and explain why both current models describe the same circuit action.
Select two materials and sort them into conductor or insulator categories.
Open the circuit and identify which path element stopped current.
completion: Learner correctly identifies every circuit part.
Learner restores the circuit to a working state.
